metal.root_cause: Model structs are hardcoded to Wgpu backend (e.g. `rope: RoPE<Wgpu>`, `norm: RmsNorm<Wgpu>`, `tok_embeddings: Tensor<Wgpu, 2>`). Metal's CubeBackend uses u32 for its integer element type parameter; wgpu uses u8. The mismatch propagates to every struct field and method signature that references these types. Fix requires making all model structs generic over B: Backend and replacing Wgpu type annotations with the generic B.
metal.fix_estimate: Refactor all model structs in src/gguf/ to be generic over B: Backend. Medium effort — mechanical change but large surface area.

note: wgpu measured 2026-04-13 on Apple M-series, --profile bench (no LTO), euler_steps=4, 10 phrases. Metal backend blocked by non-generic model structs — see metal.root_cause.
conclusion: Metal benchmark cannot run until model structs are made generic over B: Backend. The wgpu WGSL shader path (RTF avg 6.63x) is currently the only working GPU path on macOS. Auto-disable threshold (5.0x) is NOT met — TTS remains disabled on this hardware until Metal is fixed or wgpu RTF improves.
